Defendant Bank Of America, Na'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, NA'S Demurrer To Complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first, second and third cause of action on the ground that Plaintiff was properly charged for research is overruled. The Personal Schedule of Fees is ambiguous. Plaintiff has alleged a reasonably susceptible interpretation, which is sufficient to withstand demurrer. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239. Defendant has not shown that any of Plaintiff's claims are preempted. The demurrer to the third cause of action on the grounds that Defendant cannot convert Plaintiff's funds is sustained without leave to amend. The money deposited with the bank immediately became the property of Defendant and therefore cannot have been converted as a matter of law. When the fee was deducted is immaterial. See Arnold v. San Ramon Valley Bank (1921) 184 Cal. 632, 634. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action on the grounds that Plaintiff lacks standing to prosecute this claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead, if he can do so in good faith, actual injury resulting from the failure to provide the requested documents. The Court signed the order that was emailed to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, NA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued on the Court's own motion to March 1, 2013 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must immediately deliver courtesy copies of his opposition to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. The Court has a courtesy copy of the motion and reply.
